# Replay

> Local, DOM-based browser session replay for agent-driven coding sessions.

Replay captures the exact browser session a coding agent (e.g. OpenAI Codex) drives to
reproduce or verify a bug — every click, typed input, tab switch, and navigation — as a
featherweight DOM timeline. It auto-cuts the "dead air" where the agent thinks, and returns
a shareable replay link plus a portable `.replay` artifact. Built end-to-end at
[OpenAI Build Week](https://openai.devpost.com/) with **GPT-5.6 Terra** as the model in the
driver's seat; OpenAI's credits and early access made it possible.

## The problem
"It works on my machine." A bug ticket arrives with steps and a screenshot — but never what
the agent actually did in the browser to reproduce it. Reviewers can't see the reproduction,
so they can't trust the fix. Raw screen recordings are ~90% dead air because agents pause
for long stretches to think between actions.

## How it works
1. The agent drives a managed Chrome (Playwright over the Chrome DevTools Protocol). Replay
   observes the same browser — it never proxies or interferes with the agent's workflow.
2. Every DOM event, tab change, and navigation is recorded as a lightweight timeline. The
   agent drops atomic markers ("Submitting checkout", "Verified ✓") bound to the exact action
   at capture time.
3. Stopping finalizes a portable `.replay` artifact and hands back a replay URL. Idle gaps
   where the agent thought are auto-cut, so reviewers watch the seconds that matter.

## Key features
- DOM-based, not video — featherweight, pixel-faithful, inspectable, no giant recordings
- Dead-air auto-cut with three treatments: cut, fast-forward (8×), or keep
- Narrative markers bound atomically to the actions they describe
- Portable, checksummed `.replay` artifacts (offline, archivable, verifiable)
- One shareable link to a hosted player — reviewers install nothing
- Tabs, focus changes, and navigation modeled on a single shared timeline
- In-player "Ask AI" assistant that can seek, pause, and highlight elements — powered by GPT-5.6 Terra
- Local-first capture; passwords masked; the only network hop is the optional upload you choose

## Built with
GPT-5.6 Terra · rrweb · Playwright · Model Context Protocol (MCP) · Chrome DevTools Protocol · React · Vite

## Audience
Developers and reviewers who need to see — and trust — what an autonomous coding agent did
in the browser to reproduce a bug or verify a fix.

## Links
- Website: https://replaythis.io/
- Source: https://github.com/that-one-arab/replay
- Hackathon (OpenAI Build Week): https://openai.devpost.com/
